titleStmt.title: The Ambassador in Turkey ( Wilson ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Ambassador in Turkey ( Wilson ) to the Secretary of State
div.p: 1421. While I have not as yet asked anyone at FonOff what they think of our proposal concerning Straits, I have been told by various well-informed Turks, including newspapermen and a deputy who is a member of Foreign Affairs Committee of National Assembly, that proposal has been very favorably received by Turkish Government. This afternoon parliamentary group of People’s Party is meeting to hear explanation by Foreign Minister of our proposal.In his telegram 1423, November 8, 11 a.m., Ambassador Wilson reported to the Department that “PriMin said to me last night he finds our proposal for revision Montreux Convention ‘excellent’ and he is ‘greatly pleased’ with it. He added that he believed British ‘are in no hurry’ regarding this matter.” (767.68119/11–845)
Sent Dept as 1421; repeated to London as 122 and Moscow as 97.
